title: salix planifolia var. pennata
intro: Salix planifolia var. pennata, also known as the narrow-leaved willow or Alaskan willow, is a plant species within the willow family that is native to the high-altitude mountainous regions of North America. This plant contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which has been used for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The medicinal properties of Salix planifolia var. pennata have been found to help alleviate symptoms associated with fever, pain, and inflammation, providing relief for conditions such as arthritis and headaches. Traditional herbal preparations include teas, infusions, and tinctures made from the bark and leaves of the plant, which can be used internally or applied topically to promote healing and reduce discomfort.
